name: O-1 Extraordinary Ability Visa
shortDescription: The O-1 visa is for individuals who possess extraordinary ability in the sciences, arts, education, business, or athletics, or who have demonstrated extraordinary ability in the motion picture or television industry.
metaTitle: O-1 Visa Attorneys | Extraordinary Ability Visa | Watson Immigration Law
metaDescription: Expert O-1 visa counsel for extraordinary talent. Watson Immigration Law helps artists, scientists, athletes, and business leaders obtain O-1 status. Featured in NYT, Forbes, Bloomberg.
heroSubheadline: For individuals with extraordinary talent in their field. We help extraordinary ability beneficiaries obtain O-1 status and work in the U.S.

eligibility.ability: Must demonstrate extraordinary ability in field (science, arts, education, business, athletics, or film/TV)
eligibility.evidence: Must provide evidence of extraordinary ability (awards, publications, high salary, etc.)
eligibility.consultation: Must obtain advisory opinion from peer group or labor organization
eligibility.event: Must be coming to participate in specific event or activity
eligibility.benefit: Must be coming to the U.S. to continue work of extraordinary merit

duration: Initial 3-year period, extendable in 1-year increments, maximum 7 years for arts/sciences/business, no limit for athletics

faqs.question: What qualifies as 'extraordinary ability' for O-1?
faqs.answer: Extraordinary ability means a level of expertise indicating that you are among the top individuals in your field. Evidence includes receiving nationally or internationally recognized prizes/awards, membership in organizations requiring outstanding achievement, published material about you, high salary/compensation, or other substantial recognition.

faqs.question: What evidence do I need for an O-1 petition?
faqs.answer: You need at least 3 of 6 possible criteria: official academic record, professional licenses, high salary, membership in professional associations, recognition in field, other significant contributions. You also need a consultation from a peer group or labor organization, and evidence that your work will substantially benefit the U.S.

faqs.question: Can my spouse and children accompany me on an O-1 visa?
faqs.answer: Yes. Your spouse and unmarried children under 21 can accompany you as O-3 dependents. However, O-3 dependents cannot work in the U.S. — they can attend school but need separate work authorization. Your spouse cannot obtain work authorization through the O-1 petition.

faqs.question: How long can I stay on an O-1 visa?
faqs.answer: O-1 visas are issued for up to 3 years initially, with 1-year extensions possible. The maximum is 7 years for those in arts, sciences, education, business, or athletics. There is no maximum limit for those in motion picture/television or athletics who receive acclaim.

faqs.question: Can the O-1 lead to a green card?
faqs.answer: Yes, O-1 can lead to permanent residency through EB-1 (extraordinary ability) or EB-2/EB-3 employment-based categories. Many O-1 holders transition to EB-1 after demonstrating continued extraordinary ability. The O-1 provides valuable work experience and evidence for green card applications.
